title: Letter-Writing for Prairieland Defendants — International Day of Solidarity
location: Property Is Theft (P.I.T.), 411 South 5th Street, Brooklyn, NY 11211
description: NYC Anarchist Black Cross letter-writing for the Prairieland defendants on the International Day of Solidarity, after eight of nine were found guilty at the conclusion of their March 2026 trial.
body:
Join NYC Anarchist Black Cross on the International Day of Solidarity with the Prairieland Defendants for a letter-writing to eight of the nine* who were found guilty on most counts when their less-than-3-week trial ended on March 13, 2026.

All writing materials and vegan snacks will be provided. Quick ramp setup; bathroom not accessible. FREE — donations to cover the cost of stamps greatly appreciated.

* According to available documents, Meagan Morris initially cooperated, requesting interviews with law enforcement and providing information on other defendants that led to their subsequent arrests. As a result, NYC ABC does not support her.

Learn more and donate at prairielanddefendants.com.

title: Letter-Writing to Support the Prairieland Defendants
location: Property Is Theft (P.I.T.), 411 South 5th Street, Brooklyn, NY 11211
description: NYC ABC letter-writing for the Prairieland (Texas) defendants, jailed for a July 2025 noise demonstration in solidarity with ICE detainees outside the Prairieland Detention Center.
body:
The Prairieland case is a political-repression prosecution stemming from a noise demonstration in solidarity with ICE detainees that took place on July 4, 2025 outside the Prairieland Detention Center in Alvarado, Texas. The current defendants are charged as an alleged "Antifa Cell," including the transport of "antifa materials" — a box of commonly available anarchist zines.

The defendants face decades or even life behind bars, and in the case of green card holder Daniel "Des" Sanchez Estrada, also deportation. They are currently being held in a county jail while awaiting trial, scheduled to begin February 23, 2026. Beyond destroying these defendants' lives, this case could have wide-ranging impacts: making it an act of "domestic terrorism" to resist fascism, whether with organizing, solidarity, or words.

For more details: prairielanddefendants.com · @dfwsupportcommittee · freedes.net · @free.des.revol

NYC ABC encourages people to wear an N95/KN95 mask. Letter-writing supplies, vegan snacks, and info will be provided.

title: Send Love Through the Walls 2025 — Holiday Card-Writing Party
description: Annual holiday card-writing party for U.S.-held political prisoners, prisoners of war, and prisoners of conscience, co-hosted by Resistance in Brooklyn and NYC Anarchist Black Cross.
body:
In what many prisoners have told us is their favorite event of the year, Resistance in Brooklyn and NYC Anarchist Black Cross join forces to bring you the annual holiday card-writing party for U.S.-held political prisoners, prisoners of war, and prisoners of conscience. This event is always a lot of fun, the food outstanding, the camaraderie lively, and the handmade cards flat out amazing.

Updates on PP/POW campaigns will be shared. Crayons, colored pencils, paper and envelopes will be provided.

This is a hybrid event with a Zoom component for folks unable to make it in person. For the Zoom meeting information, contact Resistance in Brooklyn at resistanceinbrooklyn.ows@gmail.com. FREE — donations to cover the cost of stamps greatly appreciated.

title: NYE Noise Demo Against the Prison Industrial Complex (Brooklyn)
location: Metropolitan Detention Center (MDC), 29th Street between 2nd and 3rd Avenues, Brooklyn, NY 11232
description: New Year's Eve noise demonstration outside the MDC federal prison in Sunset Park, Brooklyn, in solidarity with political prisoners and prisoners of war.
body:
On the noisiest night of the year in New York City, come help remind the folks locked up that they are not alone. NYC Anarchist Black Cross, in response to an international call for noise demonstrations outside prisons, is asking folks to join outside the Metropolitan Detention Center (MDC) in Sunset Park, Brooklyn.

Come not to appeal to authority or speak truth to power, but rather to stand with comrades at a safe distance and show direct solidarity to those on the other side of the wall.

The state, writ large, is targeting anarchists all across the United States and abroad. This will be both protest and celebration. Bring noisemakers, air horns, drums — anything loud. D/N/R to 36th Street or R to 25th Street.

title: Running Down the Walls 2025 (NYC) — 26th anniversary
location: Prospect Park — Lincoln Road / East Lake Drive (east of the Terrace Bridge), Brooklyn, NY
description: 26th annual Running Down the Walls 5K run/walk/jog/bike to raise funds for U.S. political prisoners and the Anarchist Black Cross Federation Warchest, with sister runs in cities and prison yards across the country.
body:
NYC Anarchist Black Cross presents Running Down the Walls 2025 — the 26th anniversary of Running Down the Walls since it was started by ABCF-LA. Each year, prisoners and supporters of political prisoners organize solidarity events with Running Down the Walls.

Sister runs are held simultaneously in Albuquerque (NM), Arcata (CA), Ashland (OR), Bellefonte (PA), Boston (MA), Buffalo (NY), Chico (CA), Denver (CO), Elmore (AL), Inez (KY), Los Angeles (CA), Marion (IL), Minneapolis (MN), New York (NY), USP Navasota (TX), Pelican Bay (CA), Phoenix (AZ), Tucson (AZ), Seattle (WA), and Toronto, Ontario.

Stay after to enjoy a vegan BBQ, served taking every COVID precaution. \$20 registration (participants encouraged to get sponsors); \$30 includes a limited letterpress poster; \$50 includes the poster and an exclusive t-shirt.

title: Letter Writing for Anti-ICE Arrestees
description: Letter-writing in support of people facing repression for standing up to ICE — wheelchair-accessible space with a gender-neutral toilet.
body:
Cruising through the heat of a summer hot enough to melt ICE, join NYC ABC as we come together to support folks facing repression from the state for standing up to the USA's very own Gestapo.

All materials will be provided, as well as background on who we are writing to and guidelines to take the stress off putting pen to paper. Writing a letter to a political prisoner or prisoner of war is a concrete way to support those imprisoned for their political struggles.

If you're joining without having written to a political prisoner before, NYC ABC's guidelines are at nycabc.wordpress.com/write-a-letter. Interference Archive is on the ground floor, is wheelchair accessible, and has a gender-neutral toilet. FREE.

title: Letter Writing for June 11th Prisoners (International Day of Solidarity with Marius Mason and Long-Term Anarchist Prisoners)
description: Letter-writing for the June 11th International Day of Solidarity with Marius Mason and long-term anarchist political prisoners, including earth and animal liberation prisoners.
body:
NYC ABC responds to the June 11th call for solidarity with long-term anarchist political prisoners, while recognizing the history of this day by including earth and animal liberation prisoners. In addition to encouraging people to write to those captured by the state, we hold space for those we have lost in recent decades from those movements.

From the June 11th call: "Spring is unfolding, and the time has again come to look towards June 11th, the International Day of Solidarity with Marius Mason and Long-Term Anarchist Prisoners. While our celebration of this day is to lend attention to Marius and other anarchist prisoners at risk of being forgotten because of their long sentences, we're also continually thinking about how to emphasize how integral prisoners are, and an anti-prison struggle as a whole is, on our path towards freedom."

Letter-writing guidelines for first-timers are at nycabc.wordpress.com/write-a-letter. FREE — donations to cover stamps greatly appreciated.

title: NYE Noise Demo Against the Prison Industrial Complex (Brooklyn) — 2024
location: Metropolitan Detention Center (MDC), 29th Street between 2nd and 3rd Avenues, Brooklyn, NY 11232
description: New Year's Eve noise demonstration outside the MDC federal prison in Sunset Park, Brooklyn, in solidarity with political prisoners and prisoners of war.
body: On the noisiest night of the year in New York City, NYC Anarchist Black Cross, in response to an international call for noise demonstrations outside prisons, gathered outside the Metropolitan Detention Center (MDC) in Sunset Park, Brooklyn — to stand with comrades at a safe distance and show direct solidarity to those on the other side of the wall.

title: Send Love Through the Walls 2024 — Holiday Card-Writing Party (Queens)
description: Annual holiday card-writing party for U.S.-held political prisoners, prisoners of war, and prisoners of conscience, co-hosted by Resistance in Brooklyn and NYC Anarchist Black Cross.
body: The 2024 edition of the annual holiday card-writing party for U.S.-held political prisoners, prisoners of war, and prisoners of conscience, co-hosted by Resistance in Brooklyn and NYC Anarchist Black Cross. Crayons, colored pencils, paper and envelopes were provided. Hybrid in-person + Zoom format.
